title: Iterator.next() Methods Should Throw NoSuchElementException
description: Ensure to throw a NoSuchElementException when there are no more elements in the iteration while using the java.util.Iterator.next() method. Not doing so is non-compliant with the API contract and may cause unexpected behavior.
recommendation: Always throw a NoSuchElementException when there are no more elements in the iteration while using java.util.Iterator.next() method.
impact: Not following this rule can lead to unexpected behavior in the application due to non-compliance with the API contract.

checks.message: Always throw a NoSuchElementException when there are no more elements in the iteration while using java.util.Iterator.next() method.
